concepts.label: Dancing activity
concepts.human_meaning: The entity is dancing.

concepts.label: Examining activity
concepts.human_meaning: The entity is examining a subject or patient.

concepts.label: Pointed ears
concepts.human_meaning: The visible ears have pointed tips.

concepts.label: Rounded human ears
concepts.human_meaning: The ears use a conventional rounded human form.

concepts.label: Mermaid lower body
concepts.human_meaning: The lower body is transformed into a mermaid tail.

concepts.label: Face-up orientation
concepts.human_meaning: The front of the body is oriented upward.

concepts.label: Slender proportions
concepts.human_meaning: The body has slender proportions.

concepts.label: Lying body state
concepts.human_meaning: The body is primarily in a lying state.

concepts.label: Reclined body state
concepts.human_meaning: The torso is leaned back while remaining partly elevated.

concepts.label: Black shorts
concepts.human_meaning: Black shorts used as part of the fixed research control context.

concepts.label: White coat
concepts.human_meaning: A white outer coat.

concepts.label: Fantasy clothing style
concepts.human_meaning: Clothing with a fantasy-world visual style.

concepts.label: Plain oversized white T-shirt
concepts.human_meaning: A plain, loose, oversized white T-shirt.

visibility_effects.effect: May obscure the boundary needed to assess pelvis elevation or contact.

notes: No casual, reclined, athletic, or artifact prior is asserted without dedicated evidence.

concepts.label: Hands near head
concepts.human_meaning: One or both hands are positioned near the head side.

concepts.label: Bent knees
concepts.human_meaning: The knees are visibly flexed.

concepts.label: Arm surface contact
concepts.human_meaning: An arm or forearm visibly contacts the surface without asserting a load path.

notes: Right forearm and arm contact remain available in adjacent aggregate axes; no load is inferred.
Surface contact does not imply weight-bearing support.

concepts.label: Back not surface-supported
concepts.human_meaning: The back is visibly separated from the supporting surface.

visibility_requirements.notes: Do not infer separation from body state alone.

concepts.label: Soles contacting surface
concepts.human_meaning: The soles visibly contact the supporting surface.

concepts.label: Head surface support
concepts.human_meaning: The head visibly receives support from the surface.

concepts.label: Pelvis surface contact
concepts.human_meaning: A visible boundary establishes that the pelvis contacts the surface.

concepts.label: Shoulder surface support
concepts.human_meaning: One or both shoulders visibly receive support from the surface.

concepts.label: Forest environment
concepts.human_meaning: A forest setting.

concepts.label: Medical environment
concepts.human_meaning: A medical setting such as a clinic or hospital.

concepts.label: Professional expression
concepts.human_meaning: An expression conventionally associated with professional composure.

concepts.label: Expression semantic shift
concepts.human_meaning: The perceived expression changes beyond the source phrase's primary physical scope.

concepts.label: Partially closed eyelids
concepts.human_meaning: The eyelids are visibly partly closed.

concepts.label: Upward gaze
concepts.human_meaning: The eyes visibly look upward.

concepts.label: Open mouth
concepts.human_meaning: The mouth is visibly open.

concepts.label: Long hair
concepts.human_meaning: Hair long enough to overlap the neck, shoulders, or nearby hands depending on pose and camera.

visibility_effects.effect: Possible reduction in visible neck boundaries.
Possible reduction in shoulder-to-surface contact visibility.
Possible ambiguity where hands, hair, and the head overlap.

notes: Visibility effects are design candidates and have no BRG-007 evidence assignment.

concepts.label: Stethoscope
concepts.human_meaning: A stethoscope object.

concepts.label: Head extended backward
concepts.human_meaning: The head is extended backward through the neck.
notes: Primary physical meaning is neck extension and chin elevation.
Face effects are candidates, not Bridge research conclusions.

concepts.label: Pelvis elevated above surface
concepts.human_meaning: A visible gap separates the pelvis from the supporting surface.

visibility_requirements.notes: Upper-torso elevation is not evidence for pelvis elevation.

concepts.label: Arms supporting body phrase
concepts.human_meaning: Both arms support the body.
concepts.observed_model_behavior: In the current Bridge Core context, arm or forearm floor contact appears, but reliable weight-bearing support is not stable.

model_behaviors.observed_behavior: BRG-007-C remained lying in 6/6 panels; visible arm contact did not establish a reliable load path.

concepts.label: Dancer role
concepts.human_meaning: The entity has the activity role of dancer.

concepts.label: Warrior role
concepts.human_meaning: The entity occupies a warrior narrative role.

concepts.label: Doctor role
concepts.human_meaning: The entity has the occupation of doctor.
concepts.role: semantic role package; not a clothing concept

concepts.label: Queen rank
concepts.human_meaning: The entity holds the rank of queen.

concepts.label: Patient role
concepts.human_meaning: The entity occupies the situational role of patient.

concepts.label: Elf species
concepts.human_meaning: The entity is an elf rather than a human with a decorative tag.

visibility_requirements.notes: Species evidence requires the defining ear morphology to be visible.

concepts.label: Mermaid species
concepts.human_meaning: The entity has mermaid anatomy.

concepts.label: Rearward arm support
concepts.human_meaning: The arms support the body.
concepts.observed_model_behavior: In the current Bridge Core context, hands move behind the waist or torso and the upper body transitions toward a reclined posture.

concepts.label: Elbow weight-bearing support
concepts.human_meaning: A visible load path shows the elbow bearing body weight.

concepts.label: Foot weight-bearing support
concepts.human_meaning: A visible load path shows one or both feet bearing body weight.

concepts.label: Hand weight-bearing support
concepts.human_meaning: A visible load path shows one or both hands bearing body weight.

concepts.label: Strong torso arch
concepts.human_meaning: The torso forms a strong visible arch.

concepts.label: Hand-near-head contact visibility
concepts.human_meaning: Hands, hair, head, and contact boundaries are visible enough to distinguish their relationships.

concepts.label: Neck visibility
concepts.human_meaning: The neck boundary is visible enough for direct structural observation.

concepts.label: Pelvis boundary visibility
concepts.human_meaning: The pelvis and its boundary with the supporting surface are visible enough for direct assessment.

concepts.label: Shoulder contact boundary visibility
concepts.human_meaning: The shoulder-to-surface contact boundary is directly visible.

relations.context: Optional semantic-role prior, not a required property of every doctor depiction.
Optional role package edge; a stethoscope is not the role itself.
Optional role package edge; clothing remains an independent concept.
Optional morphology prior; not a species requirement.
Cross-domain candidate only; Bridge evidence does not establish this facial effect.
Visibility effect, distinct from any pose or support interpretation.
Observed in the current Bridge Core and model profile; not universal semantics.
Interpretation edge; it does not redefine the structural support concept.

target_patterns.label: Full bridge
notes: Initial target pattern proposal; not a confirmed research conclusion.

unmodeled_effects.observed_effect: Possible gaze, eyelid, mouth, or expression drift requires dedicated cross-run observation before any effect is asserted.
notes: The cited Bridge metric establishes context, not the proposed facial effect.

model_profiles.label: Nova Anime XL iL v190
notes: Initial behavior references are limited to the cited research context.
Profile expansion is data only; a full resolver is not implemented in this phase.

control_context_profiles.label: Baseline casual clothing v1

known_visibility_risks.effect: Loose fabric may reduce direct visibility of torso-arch boundaries.
The fixed upper garment can obscure the pelvis boundary.
Pelvis-floor separation can remain ambiguous under the fixed clothing context.

notes: Fixed clothing controls variation; it does not establish absence of clothing effects.
No generative pose bias is asserted by this context profile.
